title: SpinorRepresentationSpec
description: Specification of spinor representation, Clifford algebra conventions, chirality, and conjugation for Phase IV fermionic computations.

spinorSpecId.description: Unique identifier for this spinor representation spec

cliffordSignatureId.description: Clifford algebra signature identifier (e.g. lorentz-like-v1, euclidean-v1)

blockDimensions.description: Block dimension parameters for spinor components

spinorComponents.description: Total spinor dimension = 2^floor(dimY/2) over the complex field

chiralitySplit.description: Number of chiral components per chirality sector (= spinorComponents/2). Only meaningful when ySpaceDimension is even.

ySpaceDimension.description: Dimension of the observerse Y used for Clifford algebra construction. Determines spinor size (2^floor(dimY/2)) and whether chirality is nontrivial.

chiralityApplicable.description: Whether a nontrivial chirality operator exists. True iff ySpaceDimension is even. When false, the product of all gammas is proportional to the identity and spinors do NOT decompose into Weyl sectors. This MUST be set correctly — chirality analysis is skipped when false.

metricSignatureOnY.description: Metric signature convention on Y: Riemannian (all +), Lorentzian, or mixed. Controls the form of the fermionic inner product and the reality/hermiticity properties of the gamma matrices. MUST be a branch parameter.

gaugeRepresentationId.description: How the gauge group acts on spinors: a branch parameter. E.g. 'adjoint-v1' (rho(T_a)_bc = f^c_ab via structure constants). Determines the gauge-coupled term omega_mu^a * rho(T_a) in the Dirac operator and the total spinor-with-gauge block size per cell = spinorDim * dimG.

insertedAssumptionIds.description: List of Phase IV inserted assumption IDs relevant to this spec

passed.description: Whether the Clifford algebra anti-commutation relations were satisfied

anticommutatorMaxResidual.description: Maximum residual from anti-commutator check {gamma_mu, gamma_nu} = 2 g_{mu nu} I
